events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes "orange cable handling violations" in high-voltage safety work on an automotive production floor. Orange cables in automotive HV systems are high-voltage cables. Handling violations suggest improper handling, routing, or protection of HV cables.

This is a compliance failure. Orange HV cables have specific handling requirements - proper bend radius, support, protection from chafing, no kinking, proper routing away from sharp edges, etc. Violations mean non-compliant work.

Decision: fail
Risk: high (HV safety violations can lead to arc flash, electrocution, short circuits)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: non-compliant (and possibly installed-defective if the cables were damaged during handling)
Percent complete: rough-complete stage, maybe 70-80%
Confidence: high given clear violation described
